RESPONSE RE: DEFENDANTS PRIYA SANGER AND MICHAEL SANGERS PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION (TRANSACTION ID # 59382517) FILED BY DEFENDANT OLD REPUBLIC TITLE COMPANY

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT OLD REPUBLIC TITLE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE COURT CONFIRMS THE TENTATIVE RULING. OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF NOW ADEQUATELY PLEADS FACTS FOR THE DELAYED DISCOVERY RULE. (SEE FOX V. ETHICON ENDO-SURGERY, INC. (2005) 35 CAL.4TH 797, 808-09.; SAC PARA. 53-65.) I GRANT DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE, BUT CANNOT RESOLVE CONTESTED FACTUAL ISSUES VIA THE JUDICIAL NOTICE PROCEDURE. (SEE RICHTEK USA, INC. V. UPI SEMICONDUCTOR CORP. (2015) 242 CAL.APP.4TH 651, 660-61.) SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OR A TRIAL COULD RESOLVE WHEN PLAINTIFF WAS PLACED ON INQUIRY NOTICE AND WHETHER SHE ACTED REASONABLY IN NOT REVIEWING THE PARTIES' TENANTS-IN-COMMON AGREEMENT AND/OR BY RELYING ON STATEMENTS REGARDING THAT AGREEMENT'S TERMS. (SEE SAC PARA. 27.) WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: RICHARD ULMER; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: LAURA MARTINEZ, CSR #11332

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-11-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT OLD REPUBLIC TITLE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT is OVERRULED. Plaintiff now adequately pleads facts for the delayed discovery rule. (See Fox v. Ethicon Endo-Surgery, Inc. (2005) 35 Cal.4th 797, 808-09.; SAC para. 53-65.) I grant defendant's request for judicial notice, but cannot resolve contested factual issues via the judicial notice procedure. (See Richtek USA, Inc. v. uPI Semiconductor Corp. (2015) 242 Cal.App.4th 651, 660-61.) Summary adjudication or a trial could resolve when plaintiff was placed on inquiry notice and whether she acted reasonably in not reviewing the parties' tenants-in-common agreement and/or by relying on statements regarding that agreement's terms. (See SAC para. 27.) Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Laura Martinez, CSR #11332, Bus. Tel: 415-551-3745, email: lmartinez@sftc.org. (D302)

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT FIRST REPUBLIC BANK'S DEMURRER to the seventh and eleventh causes of action in the first amended complaint is sustained without leave to amend as to the seventh cause of action and overruled as to the eleventh cause of action. The seventh cause of action for cancellation of the deed of trust fails to allege cognizable grounds for cancellation of the deed of trust and appears to be time-barred. There does not appear to be a reasonable possibility that Ms. Ahn is able to allege a legal basis for the cancellation of the deed of trust or that she was unaware of the facts supporting her claim until a date within the limitations period. The eleventh cause of action for declaratory relief adequately alleges the need and legal basis for a declaration with regard to Ms. Ahn's obligations, if any, to the Bank.
